title: Usage Decision
learning_objectives: Understand the purpose of Usage Decision in SAP QM.
Understand stock posting after Usage Decision.
Learn important SAP Usage Decision transaction codes.
Understand the impact of Usage Decision on inventory and production.

introduction.title: What is Usage Decision?
introduction.content: Usage Decision (UD) is the final step of the quality inspection process in SAP QM. After inspection results have been recorded and evaluated, the quality inspector decides whether the inspected material should be accepted, rejected, sent for rework or scrapped. This decision determines the future status of the material in inventory.

business_purpose.title: Why is Usage Decision Important?
business_purpose.content: Usage Decision ensures that only materials meeting quality standards are released for production, storage or customer delivery. It prevents defective materials from entering the manufacturing process or reaching customers.

usage_decision_types.description: Material meets all quality requirements and is released for unrestricted use.
Material fails inspection and cannot be used.
Material requires additional processing before it can be accepted.
Material is unsuitable for use and must be scrapped.
Incoming material is returned to the supplier due to quality issues.

step_by_step_procedure: Select the appropriate Usage Decision code.
Enter accepted, rejected or rework quantity if applicable.

stock_postings.description: Accepted material becomes available for production or sales.
Rejected material is blocked from use.
Material is removed from usable inventory.
Material is prepared for return to the supplier.

system_updates: Quality reports reflect the final decision.

integration.purpose: Updates inventory stock after the Usage Decision.
Accepted materials become available for production or storage.
Accepted finished goods become available for customer delivery.
Inventory valuation is updated based on stock movements.

business_example.title: Example
business_example.content: A batch of stainless steel coils completes inspection. Thickness, width, hardness and surface finish all meet the required specifications. The quality engineer records an 'Accepted' Usage Decision in QA11, and SAP automatically moves the material from Quality Inspection stock to Unrestricted Use stock, making it available for warehouse storage or customer dispatch.

best_practices: Review all inspection results before making the Usage Decision.
Record decisions immediately after inspection completion.
Document reasons for rejection or rework.
common_errors: Selecting the wrong Usage Decision code.
Posting accepted material as blocked stock.
Incomplete inspection results before Usage Decision.
important_notes: A Usage Decision should only be made after all mandatory inspection characteristics have been recorded.
The Usage Decision directly affects inventory availability.
Rejected materials should follow the organization's non-conformance process.
jsl_notes: Only authorized quality personnel should record the Usage Decision.
Rejected materials must be handled according to JSL quality procedures.
Review plant-specific approval requirements before releasing critical materials.
